q: Given the choice of anyone in the world, whom would you want as a dinner guest?
Would you like to be famous? In what way?
Before making a phone call, do you ever rehearse what you're going to say?
What would constitute a perfect day for you?
options: Quality time with people I love

q: When did you last sing to yourself? To someone else?
If you were able to live to the age of 90 and retain either the mind or body of a 30-year-old, which would you want?
options: Body — I'd figure the rest out

q: Do you have a secret hunch about how you will die?
options: I try not to think about it

q: Name three things you and your partner appear to have in common.
For what in your life do you feel most grateful?
options: The people who chose to stay

q: If you could change anything about the way you were raised, what would it be?
options: Nothing — it made me who I am

q: Take four minutes and tell your partner your life story in as much detail as possible.
options: I'd skip to the turning points
I'd focus on who shaped me
I'd struggle to know where to start

q: If you could wake up tomorrow having gained any one quality or ability, what would it be?
If a crystal ball could tell you the truth about yourself, your life, the future, or anything else, what would you want to know?
Is there something that you've dreamed of doing for a long time? Why haven't you done it?
What is the greatest accomplishment of your life?
What do you value most in a friendship?
What is your most treasured memory?
options: Something I almost forgot until now

q: What is your most terrible memory?
options: A loss I wasn't ready for
A betrayal I didn't see coming
A moment I can't take back

q: If you knew that in one year you would die suddenly, would you change anything about the way you are now living? Why?
options: I'd tell people how I really feel
Honestly? Not much — I'd just be more present

q: What does friendship mean to you?
options: Someone who sees the real me
A safe space to fall apart

q: What roles do love and affection play in your life?
options: Everything — I need it to function
Important, but I struggle to show it
I give more than I receive
I'm still learning how to let it in

q: Alternate sharing something you consider a positive characteristic of your partner. Share a total of five items.
options: Their honesty, even when it's hard

q: How close and warm is your family? Do you feel your childhood was happier than most other people's?
options: Not close — I built my own family
Happy enough, but I don't compare

q: How do you feel about your relationship with your mother?
Make three true 'we' statements each. For instance, 'We are both in this room feeling...'
options: We're both more open than usual
We both want this to mean something
We're both pretending to be chill

q: Complete this sentence: 'I wish I had someone with whom I could share...'
options: The silence without it being awkward

q: If you were going to become a close friend with your partner, what would be important for them to know?
options: I take a while to fully trust
I care more than I show
I need space but I'll always come back

q: Tell your partner what you like about them; be very honest this time, saying things you might not say to someone you've just met.
options: You listen like you actually care
I like that you're not trying too hard

q: Share with your partner an embarrassing moment in your life.
options: A public fail I still cringe at
A text I wish I never sent
Something no one else knows about

q: When did you last cry in front of another person? By yourself?
options: I cry alone, never in front of others

q: Tell your partner something that you like about them already.
options: The way you make me think

q: What, if anything, is too serious to be joked about?
If you were to die this evening with no opportunity to communicate with anyone, what would you most regret not having told someone? Why haven't you told them yet?
options: That I'm proud of who they became

q: Your house, containing everything you own, catches fire. After saving your loved ones and pets, you have time to safely make a final dash to save any one item. What would it be? Why?
Of all the people in your family, whose death would you find most disturbing? Why?
options: A sibling — they know me best
I can't even think about this

q: Share a personal problem and ask your partner's advice on how they might handle it. Also, ask how your partner thinks you feel about the problem.
options: A fear I haven't faced yet
